content:
Relocating to the new Bishoftu Airport City hub: what commercial logistics businesses need to know

Logistics businesses follow airport city hub—warehouses, freight forwarders, customs brokers need commercial relocation matching aviation timelines: large bay doors, forklift paths, IT for tracking systems, and staff housing south corridor parallel.

This guide relocates commercial logistics operations to Bishoftu airport city hub zone.

Site selection logistics criteria

  • Truck turning radius 16m+ verify.
  • Loading dock count and height.
  • Power three-phase warehouse.
  • Fiber redundancy aviation SLA.
  • Staff commute bus plan from Addis.

FF&E and warehouse move

Pallet racking disassemble labeled; WMS servers IT parallel; forklift transport licensed. Inventory staged minimize downtime—Saturday wave common.

Regulatory

Business license zone update; customs proximity benefit paperwork not automatic—verify authority jurisdiction.

Cross-zone move from Addis

Truck licensing route plan; multi-trip racking; employee shuttle first week retention.

Active Movers commercial hub package

Warehouse survey; multi-truck coordination; Bishoftu route licensed fleet.

Conclusion

Airport city hub logistics businesses relocate with dock-capable sites, IT-WMS parallelism, licensed cross-zone trucks, and staff commute realism—commercial move succeeds when warehouse operational not merely keys handed.
author: Active Movers Team
